title: acetosella gardneriana
intro: Acetosella gardneriana, also known as the New Zealand cranberry or tangleberry, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It is native to New Zealand and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and urinary tract problems due to its high concentration of vitamin C and other antioxidants. The plant has been used to treat conditions such as scurvy, diarrhea, and kidney stones, and has also been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Acetosella gardneriana can be prepared as a tea or tincture for medicinal use, and its berries can be consumed fresh or used in jams and preserves.
